title: Does Asuka sound different to anyone in 222
display_title: Does Asuka sound different to anyone in 222
wikitext:
[]: Does Asuka sound different to anyone in 2.22?             Quote Post by Boethiah » Sat Jan 28, 2017 12:06 pm She sounds different from the series somehow. Her voice is less harsh, less grating. This is weird because I know that's still Tiffany Grant, and she sounds the same as she does in the series in 3.33.

[]: Re: Does Asuka sound different to anyone in 2.22?             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Jan 29, 2017 12:07 pm Your voice changes as you age. Eva 2.22 was dubbed in late 2010/early 2011, which would make it 7-11 years since she recorded for D&R, EoE and the director's cuts. When I first saw the dub of Eva 1.11, I was pretty sure they pitched up Spike Spencer to try to compensate. Perhaps they did that for Tiffany Grant as well. Also, recording technology has gotten a bit better, so the recordings might be cleaner, with less distortion that can cause the vocals to seem harsher than they are.

[]: Re: Does Asuka sound different to anyone in 2.22?             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Jan 29, 2017 12:07 pm Your voice changes as you age. Eva 2.22 was dubbed in late 2010/early 2011, which would make it 7-11 years since she recorded for D&R, EoE and the director's cuts. When I first saw the dub of Eva 1.11, I was pretty sure they pitched up Spike Spencer to try to compensate. Perhaps they did that for Tiffany Grant as well. Also, recording technology has gotten a bit better, so the recordings might be cleaner, with less distortion that can cause the vocals to seem harsher than they are.
